seo.title: Automatic Lyrics Transcription — AI Song-to-Text Tool
seo.description: Automatic lyrics transcription with AI: get beat-synced lyrics from any song or video link, then export TXT, LRC, or SRT in 50+ languages. Free to start.

hero.badge: Automatic lyrics transcription from any song or video
hero.h1: Automatic Lyrics Transcription — AI Song-to-Text Tool
hero.subhead: Get accurate, timestamped lyrics from any uploaded song audio. Upload audio, AI transcribes lyrics automatically, syncs every line to the beat, then lets you preview and export as TXT, LRC, or SRT — free, no subscription needed.

trust: Export free, no subscription or credit card

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drop an MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A audio file (up to 100MB). No account needed to start, and no manual lyric typing required. The AI listens to the vocal performance and does the transcription for you.

steps.title: AI transcribes and syncs
steps.description: AI transcribes lyrics from the audio automatically and syncs every line to the beat — in 50+ languages. Click any line to edit. It handles ad-libs, layered vocals, hooks, and fast sections that are hard to catch by ear.

steps.title: Preview and export
steps.description: Review the full transcript with beat-synced timing. Download TXT, LRC, or SRT for captions, descriptions, streaming lyrics, or subtitles — or click through to preview the same synced lyrics as a lyric video. Free to export, no subscription required.

styles.title: Three export formats for automatic lyrics transcription
styles.subtitle: Choose the file that fits your next step after AI transcribes and syncs the song.

items.name: TXT
items.description: Clean lyrics, line by line. Use TXT for descriptions, posts, docs, lyric sheets, and quick edits.

items.name: LRC
items.description: Timestamped lyrics for music players, karaoke-style previews, streaming workflows, and synced lyric displays.

items.name: SRT
items.description: Subtitle-ready lyrics for video editors, YouTube captions, Reels, Shorts, and translation workflows.

whyChoose.title: Why Transcribe Song Lyrics on LyricVideoMaker
whyChoose.description: Transcribing lyrics by ear means replaying the same line ten times, guessing slang, and rebuilding timing later. LyricVideoMaker gives you a song-first workflow: transcript, timing, export, and lyric video preview in one place.

highlight.title: A transcript that already knows the timing
highlight.description: When transcription finishes, the result is already synced. Download the lyrics from the result page, or use the same timed lines to preview a lyric video without re-uploading the song.

cards.title: Built for song-to-lyrics work
cards.description: Get readable lyric lines shaped for songs, not a generic speech transcript. Hooks, repeated choruses, ad-libs, and fast sections stay easy to review.

cards.title: Use uploaded audio files
cards.description: Upload a song file for audio-to-lyrics. Link-based video extraction is planned, but the stable workflow today starts with audio upload.

cards.title: One click from transcript to video
cards.description: After you download TXT, LRC, or SRT, keep going with Preview as Lyric Video to turn the synced transcript into a full visual result.

contentSections.heading: What is automatic lyrics transcription?
contentSections.intro: Automatic lyrics transcription extracts editable song lyrics from an audio file and adds timing so the text can become captions, LRC, SRT, or a lyric video. The stable path starts from uploaded audio, detects the language, transcribes vocals, and gives you line-level control before you reuse the result.
bullets: Input is a supported audio file rather than a video URL.
It can detect 50+ languages and produce editable lyric lines.
Output can feed a lyric video workflow and, where available, text or timed lyric formats.
It differs from speech transcription because music timing, repeated hooks, ad-libs, and sung phrasing matter.

contentSections.heading: automatic lyrics transcription specs at a glance

rows: Accepted input
Audio upload only: MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A, up to 100MB per file.
Lyrics
AI transcribes vocals automatically in 50+ languages, then lets you edit every line before export.
Sync
Beat-aware line timing keeps lyrics readable and musical instead of simply dropping captions on a fixed grid.
Visuals
Storyboard planning and AI scene-image generation create backgrounds around the mood of the song.
Exports
MP4 with original audio in 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 for YouTube, Shorts, TikTok, Reels, and feeds.
Start
Free credits are available at the start, so you can preview the workflow before spending more time on a release.

contentSections.heading: TXT, LRC, and SRT: which lyric format fits?
contentSections.intro: Transcribed lyrics become more useful when the output format matches the next job. Plain text, timed lyric files, and subtitle files solve different problems.

rows: TXT
Best for descriptions, publishing notes, lyric sheets, and quick proofreading.
LRC
Best for music-player style synced lyrics where line timestamps matter.
SRT
Best for subtitle workflows in video editors and caption review.
Lyric video draft
Best when the timing is already good enough to preview with visuals.
Manual cleanup
Best for artist names, stylized spelling, mixed-language hooks, and background vocals.
Archive copy
Keep a clean text copy even if the final video uses styled line breaks.

contentSections.heading: Tips for cleaner automatic lyrics transcription
contentSections.intro: Use these checks before you render, especially when the same project needs more than one platform format.
bullets: Upload the version with the clearest lead vocal, not necessarily the loudest master.
Scan proper nouns first; AI often needs help with names, places, and invented words.
Mark repeated hooks consistently so later video timing stays predictable.
Do not remove breaths or ad-libs automatically; they may be part of the performance.
Use line breaks to match how the phrase is sung, not how it would appear in a poem.
Review bilingual sections manually even when language detection is correct.
Turn the synced transcript into a lyric video while the timing is fresh in your mind.

contentSections.heading: Why song transcription is not ordinary speech-to-text
body: A automatic lyrics transcription helps you extract editable lyrics and timing from song audio without starting from a blank editing timeline. The current stable workflow starts with an audio file, then uses AI transcription, beat-aware lyric timing, storyboard planning, generated scene images, preview, line-by-line correction, and MP4 export so the song becomes a usable release asset faster.

For automatic lyrics transcription, the important choices happen before rendering. The table below turns language detection, line cleanup, timestamps, and reuse in a video into practical decisions you can make while the AI handles transcription, timing, and scene generation.
Use these checks before you render a automatic lyrics transcription, especially when the video will be reused across several platforms.

automatic lyrics transcription works best when it respects the order of the music. Lyrics are not decoration added at the end; they are the structure the viewer follows. By transcribing first, aligning second, and designing third, the workflow keeps the words, the beat, and the generated imagery connected instead of letting a template make every song feel the same.

The practical benefit is control without timeline labor. You still decide whether the style feels right, whether a line break should change, and whether the video needs a vertical, square, or widescreen export. The repetitive parts are handled by AI, but the final judgment stays with the person releasing the song.

That balance is especially useful for TXT, LRC, and SRT planning. It gives you a credible first version quickly, then leaves room for taste: cleaner typography, different scene energy, or a more restrained layout when the lyrics need quiet space.

A good automatic lyrics transcription also protects the listener's attention. The viewer should never have to choose between reading the line and watching the scene; the scene exists to make the lyric feel more alive. That is why contrast, line length, timing, and motion restraint matter as much as the first generated image.

The review habit is simple: watch once for words, once for rhythm, and once for platform fit. The first pass catches transcription mistakes, the second catches lines that arrive early or late, and the third catches text that works on desktop but fails on a phone or inside a social feed.

For creators who need lyrics before visuals, the real win is not only speed. It is having one source project that can become a full video, a short vertical clip, and a square feed asset without retyping lyrics or rebuilding timing. Consistency across those exports makes a release feel planned.

The safest publishing pattern is to make the lyric video while the song is still fresh in your ear. You will hear awkward line breaks faster, recognize misheard words sooner, and make better choices about where the visual energy should rise or stay quiet.

The useful boundary is that AI should remove repetitive labor, not remove taste. Let it transcribe, sync, and propose scenes; then use the preview to decide whether the song needs more space, more contrast, a calmer verse, or a stronger hook treatment.

That is also why the audio-first constraint is helpful. Starting with a clean song file keeps the workflow focused on vocals, beat, and structure before any visual decoration appears. Once those foundations are correct, the design choices become much easier to judge.

contentSections.heading: automatic lyrics transcription checklist before export
contentSections.intro: A quick pre-export review catches most quality problems while they are still cheap to fix.

rows: Audio readiness
Accepted formats are MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, and M4A; keep the file under 100MB.
Language review
The system auto-detects 50+ languages, but bilingual hooks and names deserve a manual pass.
Timing review
Beat sync gives a strong baseline; nudge any line that lands early or late against the vocal.
Scene review
Generated images should match the mood without making text hard to read.
Export review
Check 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 separately because readable text size changes by format.

items.question: Is automatic lyrics transcription free?
items.answer: Yes. Sign up, get free credits, and transcribe your song — no subscription required, no credit card needed.

items.question: How accurate is AI lyrics transcription?
items.answer: AI handles most lyrics well, including fast sections and layered vocals. You can click any line to edit if a word needs correction — the tool is built for review, not blind trust.

items.question: What languages does it support?
items.answer: 50+ languages. The AI detects the language of the vocals automatically.

items.question: Can I get lyrics from an online video link?
items.answer: Link-based extraction is planned. Today, upload the audio file from the song; AI transcribes the vocals into editable lyrics, then you can continue into a lyric video workflow.

items.question: Can I use exported lyrics or videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes — your exported lyrics and videos are yours to use. Make sure you have the rights to the music you upload.

items.question: Can I turn the transcription into a lyric video?
items.answer: Yes. Once the lyrics are reviewed, continue into a full lyric video with AI-generated visuals, beat sync, and multi-format export — all in the same flow.
